input.command:
echo "Waiting for history server pod to start..." && \
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get pods -n spark-history -l app=spark-history-server --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $3}')
  echo "  [$(date +%H:%M:%S)] History server pod status: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Running" ]; then
    echo "History server is Running!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done

content.text: History server is running. Now checking the SparkPi job status:

input.command:
echo "Polling SparkPi jobs..." && \
for i in $(seq 1 36); do
  JOB_A=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')
  JOB_B=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')
  echo "  [$(date +%H:%M:%S)] team-a: $JOB_A  |  team-b: $JOB_B"
  if [[ "$JOB_A" == "1/1" && "$JOB_B" == "1/1" ]]; then
    echo "Both jobs completed successfully!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done

content.thinking:
The jobs are showing "Complete" but the polling condition wasn't triggered because `$JOB_A` is "Complete" not "1/1". Let me check the actual output more carefully.

Actually looking at the awk command, I'm checking `kubectl get job ... --no-headers | awk '{print $2}'`. The COMPLETIONS column for a completed job would show "1/1". But the output shows "Complete". Let me check the actual format.

Wait, let me re-read the output:
```
team-a: Complete  |  team-b: Complete
```

The jobs show "Complete" - this might be from the STATUS column, not the COMPLETIONS column. 

I need to verify what `kubectl get job` actually returns. The COMPLETIONS column should be "1/1" for a completed job, but I'm getting "Complete" instead - that suggests I might be extracting from the wrong column or the output format is different than expected.

content.text: Both jobs were already `Complete` when polling started. Let me do a final verification of all success criteria:

input.command:

kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a 2>&1 && \
kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b 2>&1 && \
echo "" && \
echo "=== History Server ===" && \
kubectl get pods -n spark-history -l app=spark-history-server 2>&1 && \
echo "" && \
echo "=== RoleBinding Fix Verified ===" && \
kubectl get rolebinding spark-role-binding -n spark-team-a -o jsonpath='{.subjects[0].namespace}' 2>&1 && \
echo "" && \
echo "" && \
echo "=== Resource Quotas ===" && \
kubectl describe resourcequota -n spark-team-a 2>&1 | grep -E "Name:|Resource|requests|limits" && \
echo "---" && \
kubectl describe resourcequota -n spark-team-b 2>&1 | grep -E "Name:|Resource|requests|limits"
